subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards
- **1 main game board** — Depicts Arrakis with worker-placement spaces organized by faction affiliation (Emperor, Spacing Guild, Bene Gesserit, Fremen) plus neutral Arrakis locations.
- **Conflict area** — Central conflict zone where combat is resolved each round.
- **4 influence tracks** — Emperor, Spacing Guild, Bene Gesserit, Fremen.

#### Cards
- **Starting deck** — 10 cards per player (identical starting decks).
- **Imperium deck** — Large market deck of cards to acquire.
- **Reserve cards** — Always-available cards (Arrakis Liaison, The Spice Must Flow).
- **Intrigue cards** — Secret cards providing combat bonuses, plot effects, or end-game VP.
- **Conflict cards** — 1 revealed per round determining combat rewards.

#### Other Components
- **Agent tokens** — 2 per player (workers placed on the board). A third can be unlocked.
- **Swordmaster** — Unlockable third agent.
- **Troops** — Combat cubes placed in the conflict area.
- **Spice tokens** — Primary currency/resource.
- **Solari tokens** — Money.
- **Water tokens** — Required for certain Arrakis actions.
- **VP markers** — Track victory points (race to 10).
- **Alliance tokens** — Gained at influence level 2 with any faction.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Board Setup
1. **Place main board** — Central board showing Arrakis locations and faction spaces.
2. **Imperium market** — Shuffle the Imperium deck. Deal 5 cards face up as the market row.
3. **Reserve cards** — Place Arrakis Liaison and The Spice Must Flow cards in the reserve (always available to buy).
4. **Conflict deck** — Shuffle conflict cards. Place the deck with conflict I cards on top, then II, then III.
5. **Intrigue deck** — Shuffle and place face down.
6. **Influence tracks** — Set all player markers at 0 on all 4 faction tracks.
7. **Mentat space** — Place the mentat token on its board space.

#### Resource Supply
- **Spice, solari, water** — Place all tokens in a general supply.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Components
- **Starting deck** — Each player takes an identical 10-card starting deck and shuffles it.
- **Draw 5** — Draw 5 cards as your opening hand.
- **Agents** — Take 2 agent tokens (workers) in your color.
- **Starting resources** — 1 water token.
- **VP marker** — Place at 0 on the VP track.
- **Troops** — Place troops in your supply (not yet deployed).

#### Leader Selection (Optional)
- **Leader cards** — Each player may select a unique leader providing an asymmetric ability.
- **Starting bonus** — Some leaders provide additional starting resources or modified starting decks.

#### First Player
- **Random selection** — Determine the first player randomly. First player token passes each round.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
#### Core Concept
Dune: Imperium is a hybrid deck-building and worker-placement game set in the Dune universe. On your turn, you play a card from your hand to send an agent (worker) to a corresponding board space. Cards not used for agent turns are revealed during combat to add strength. This dual-use of cards — placement enablers AND combat strength — creates compelling decisions about which cards to play and which to hold.

#### Game Arc
- **Race to 10 VP** — The game ends when a player reaches 10 VP, or at the end of a set number of rounds (whichever comes first).
- **Escalating conflicts** — Conflict cards increase in value as the game progresses (Conflict I → II → III).
- **Deck evolution** — Your deck improves as you acquire powerful Imperium cards.
- **Faction alliances** — Reaching influence level 2 with a faction earns an alliance token (worth 1 VP and a bonus).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Round start** — Reveal a new conflict card showing this round's combat rewards.
2. **Player turns** — In clockwise order, each player takes either an Agent Turn or a Reveal Turn.
3. **Combat** — After all players have taken their Reveal Turn, resolve combat.
4. **Makers** — Sandworm bonus (spice reward if applicable).
5. **Recall** — Return agents to players. Refresh the Imperium market.

#### Agent Turn (Play a Card + Place an Agent)
- **Play 1 card** — Choose a card from your hand. It shows which board spaces your agent can visit (icons on the card match board space icons).
- **Place agent** — Send 1 agent to a matching board space and gain its reward.
- **Card effect** — Also resolve the card's Agent box effect (if any).
- **Repeat** — You keep taking Agent Turns until you run out of agents (normally 2, up to 3 with Swordmaster).

#### Reveal Turn (End Your Round)
- **Reveal remaining hand** — Flip all remaining cards in your hand face up.
- **Reveal effects** — Each revealed card contributes its Reveal box effect (typically combat strength or persuasion for buying cards).
- **Deploy troops** — You may deploy troops from your garrison to the conflict.
- **Buy cards** — Spend persuasion (from revealed cards) to acquire cards from the Imperium market or reserve.
- **No more actions** — After your Reveal Turn, you take no more turns this round.

#### Combat Resolution
- **Combat strength** — Total your troops in the conflict zone + combat strength from revealed cards + intrigue card bonuses.
- **Ranking** — Players are ranked by total combat strength.
- **1st place** — Wins the conflict card's top reward (usually VP + bonus).
- **2nd place** — Gets the second-tier reward.
- **3rd/4th place** — Lesser or no reward.
- **Troops lost** — Troops in the conflict are returned to supply (not garrison).

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Board Spaces by Faction
- **Emperor spaces** — Gain solari, troops, and intrigue cards. Advance Emperor influence.
- **Spacing Guild spaces** — Gain spice, passage, and fold space. Advance Guild influence.
- **Bene Gesserit spaces** — Gain intrigue cards, card draw, and influence manipulation. Advance BG influence.
- **Fremen spaces** — Gain water, troops, and desert power. Advance Fremen influence.
- **Arrakis spaces** — Harvest spice (the most valuable resource). Some require water to access.

#### Faction Influence
- **4 tracks** — Emperor, Spacing Guild, Bene Gesserit, Fremen.
- **Advancement** — Gained by visiting faction-aligned board spaces.
- **Level 2 alliance** — Reaching level 2 earns an alliance token (1 VP + ongoing bonus). Only the first 2 players to reach level 2 with a faction get an alliance token.
- **Level 4** — Reaching level 4 earns a bonus VP.
- **Influence matters** — Some Imperium cards require minimum influence to acquire.

#### Deck Building
- **Persuasion** — Generated during your Reveal Turn. Spend to buy cards.
- **Imperium market** — 5 face-up cards. Refreshes each round.
- **Reserve cards** — Always available: Arrakis Liaison (cheap, gains agent), The Spice Must Flow (expensive, 1 VP each).
- **Trash mechanic** — Some effects let you remove (trash) weak starting cards to streamline your deck.

#### Spice
- **Primary resource** — Spice is used to pay costs, trade for solari, and fuel powerful card abilities.
- **Harvesting** — Arrakis board spaces yield spice. Some require water.
- **Maker bonus** — Bonus spice from the sandworm track.

#### Intrigue Cards
- **Secret hand** — Drawn from the intrigue deck, kept hidden.
- **Types** — Combat (play during combat for strength), Plot (play during your turn for effects), Endgame (score VP at game end).
- **Powerful swings** — A well-timed intrigue card can win a combat or steal a VP.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Swordmaster (Third Agent)
- **Unlock** — Pay 8 solari to unlock your Swordmaster, gaining a 3rd agent permanently.
- **Major advantage** — 3 agents vs. opponents' 2 is a significant action advantage.
- **Priority** — Acquiring the Swordmaster early is often a top priority.

#### The Spice Must Flow
- **Reserve card** — Always available to purchase for 9 persuasion.
- **VP card** — Worth 1 VP immediately upon acquisition.
- **Endgame push** — Buying these in late game is a primary VP source.

#### Mentat
- **Board space** — One player per round can claim the Mentat.
- **Benefit** — Draw 1 card and gain 1 agent for this round only (temporary 3rd agent).
- **Cost** — 2 solari.

#### Sandworms (Makers)
- **Spice bonus** — When conflict cards with sandworm icons are revealed, bonus spice is distributed.
- **Timing** — Occurs at a specific phase in the round.

#### Troops and Garrison
- **Garrison** — Troops not in combat wait in your garrison.
- **Deployment** — During your Reveal Turn, move troops from garrison to the conflict.
- **Supply** — Troops lost in combat return to your supply (must be re-recruited to garrison).

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Trigger
- **10 VP** — The game ends at the end of the round in which any player reaches 10 VP.
- **Conflict III exhaustion** — If the Conflict III deck runs out, the game also ends.

#### VP Sources (During Game)
- **Conflict wins** — VP from winning 1st or 2nd in conflicts.
- **Alliance tokens** — 1 VP each (from reaching influence level 2).
- **Influence level 4** — 1 VP for reaching level 4 with any faction.
- **The Spice Must Flow** — 1 VP each when acquired.
- **Certain Imperium cards** — Some cards grant VP when acquired or through effects.
- **Intrigue cards** — Endgame intrigue cards score VP at game end.

#### Final Scoring
- **Add endgame intrigue** — Reveal and score any endgame intrigue cards.
- **Total VP** — Sum all VP from all sources.

#### Winner
- **Highest VP** — Player with the most VP wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with most spice. Then most solari. Then most troops in garrison.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Cards serve dual purpose** — Every card played as an agent is one fewer card for combat strength. Weigh both uses.
- **Get the Swordmaster** — 3 agents is a massive advantage. Prioritize saving 8 solari early.
- **Don't ignore combat** — Conflict rewards escalate. Winning later conflicts can be worth 2-3 VP.
- **Faction influence is VP** — Alliances (level 2) and level 4 bonuses are reliable VP. Spread or focus influence.
- **Thin your deck** — Remove weak starting cards when possible. A lean deck draws your best cards more often.
- **Spice is king** — Spice enables the strongest plays. Prioritize harvesting when safe.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
#### Strategy 1: Military Dominance
- **Goal** — Win conflicts consistently for VP and rewards.
- **Key actions** — Recruit troops, gain combat intrigue cards, deploy heavily.
- **Risk** — Opponents may avoid combat, making your troops less valuable.

#### Strategy 2: Influence Runner
- **Goal** — Rush alliances and level 4 bonuses across factions.
- **Key actions** — Visit faction spaces frequently, acquire faction-aligned cards.
- **Benefit** — Reliable VP from alliance tokens without needing to win combat.

#### Strategy 3: Spice Economy
- **Goal** — Dominate spice production to fuel powerful card purchases.
- **Key actions** — Arrakis spaces, water management, spice-generating cards.
- **Benefit** — Economic engine enables buying The Spice Must Flow and expensive Imperium cards.

#### Strategy 4: Deck Engine
- **Goal** — Build a powerful deck with strong synergies for both placement and combat.
- **Key actions** — Trash weak cards, buy high-synergy Imperium cards.
- **Benefit** — A tuned deck provides both strong agent turns and overwhelming combat strength.

#### General Principles
- **Timing combat** — Don't fight every battle. Choose your conflicts based on the reward and your position.
- **Card market awareness** — Watch the Imperium market for cards that fit your strategy.
- **Endgame intrigue** — Save endgame intrigue cards for a VP burst at the end.
